commit_msg: chore: add ytpipeline placeholder for future ffmpeg video output
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Trap Door
subtitle: He left a door open and didn't name what was waiting on the other side.
when_where: 🌅 Still 9:05 AM, main branch — but now the light had shifted. A placeholder. A comment. A *future* that hadn't happened yet. 🔌 ffmpeg waiting in the wings like a hired gun, ready to turn text into video, haiku into *film*. The code wasn't here. Just the promise of it.
who_whom: 🗂️ The ytpipeline stub sat there like a loaded gun on a desk — *not yet fired*, but everyone knew it would be. Omkar wasn't building it today; he was *reserving the space* for something bigger, something that would take his three-act haiku and render it into something the world could *consume*. 📹 The placeholder was the real crime: proof he'd already decided the ending.
what_why: ⚠️ He didn't write the code; he *named the future*. A single placeholder, a comment, an empty function waiting for ffmpeg to fill it — and in that emptiness, he'd already committed to *scale*. 💭 The haiku viewer would become a haiku *factory*. 🎬 Every poem, rendered. Every moment, preserved. Every confession, turned into content. The placeholder wasn't laziness; it was *inevitability*, written in advance.
verdict: He didn't defer the work; he deferred the moment of reckoning.
